title: trichocline crispa
intro: Trichocline crispa, also known as Crisped Trichocline or New Zealand Mullein, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Trichocline crispa include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and soothe respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Trichocline crispa can be prepared as a herbal tea, infused oil, or dried flower tincture, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
